anki.deckName: Corebook::Nuclear Medicine::Genitourinary Basic::Renal Scar and Pyelonephritis

imageRegistry.caption: Tc-99m dimercaptosuccinic acid (DMSA) renal cortical scan shows normal uptake in both kidneys. Note the relatively decreased activity in the upper poles .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Posterior Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows region-of-interest analysis of bilateral kidneys and background activity , which allows for calculation of relative functioning renal mass.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: On Tc-99m DMSA scans, Mattoo et al suggest separating the kidney into 12 total segments for more consistent reporting between interpreting physicians. This is most consistently done by separating the kidney in half, and then again separating those halves into 2 quadrants. Each quadrant then has 3 segments.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows multiple foci of cortical scar in a patient with vesicoureteral reflux.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT shows multiple bilateral cortical defects in a patient with bilateral high-grade reflux and multiple previous urinary tract infections (UTIs). Fetal lobulation could also have this appearance.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan in a patient with fever, UTI, and vesicoureteral reflux shows multiple photopenic defects that likely represent pyelonephritis and scar .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows a rounded lesion in the interpolar left kidney , found to be a cyst on US.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical SPECT shows multiple cortical defects in a patient with acute pyelonephritis.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: 3D Tc-99m DMSA scan shows focally increased activity , suggesting a hypertrophied column of Bertin.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT shows enlarged central photopenia due to reflux-induced hydronephrosis and a focal defect in the superolateral cortex , consistent with scar.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Anterior Tc-99m glucoheptonate renal cortical scan in a transplant kidney shows multiple cortical defects . It is postulated that acute rejection episodes and renal infarcts cause these defects.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal scan in a left renal transplant shows thinning of cortex in the inferior pole but no frank areas of cortical scarring.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal CECT in the same patient shows minimal renal cortex remaining due to hydronephrosis , explaining the photopenic defect on DMSA.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Posterior Tc-99m DMSA scan in a patient with massive left hydronephrosis demonstrates no left renal cortical uptake , consistent with a nonfunctioning kidney. There is incidental tracer remaining in port at site of injection .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal fused F-18 FDG PET/CT shows no uptake nor excretion of F-18 FDG in the collecting system of the left kidney , concerning for nonfunctioning kidney. Note the normal right kidney appearance .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: 3D Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows normal left renal cortical uptake and a focal photopenic defect in the upper pole of the right kidney , consistent with a scar.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial CT shows a large left upper pole renal cyst . Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan shows corresponding photopenia in the left upper pole cyst .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal CECT shows acute renal infarcts in the right kidney of a patient with atrial fibrillation. Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan shows cortical defects corresponding to the infarcts.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal fused F-18 FDG PET/CT shows no uptake/excretion in the native kidneys of a patient on hemodialysis. Kidneys demonstrate numerous cysts and calcifications, consistent with polycystic kidney disease.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial F-18 FDG PET/CT in a patient with a renal transplant shows diffuse transplant uptake extending into the sinus fat , without tracer excretion. This was diagnosed as posttransplant lymphoproliferative disorder.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan shows a large cortical defect involving the upper and lower poles of the right kidney.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal MR in the same patient shows a right upper pole enhancing mass and a lower pole simple cyst . Axial MR shows the complex renal mass.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageEvidence.purpose: Visual evidence for duplicate detection. Use these image files before deciding that source images are exact duplicates.
imageEvidence.duplicateClassificationRule: Archive only images proven to be exact/same-slice/same-screenshot duplicates or unusable. Caption-only similarity is not enough; uncertain or merely similar images stay primary as recognition reinforcement.
